title: SKR03 Account Lookup Fix — unblock Purchase POST for German chart
scope: Single-file PATCH on app/api/company/[companyId]/purchases/[purchaseId]/post/route.ts. Extends hardcoded default-account lookup arrays to include SKR03 codes (3300 Wareneingang, 1600 Verbindlichkeiten) alongside existing US codes (1000/6000/2000/5000). Additive only — no removal. Unblocks the Golden POST cycle for users importing SKR03 chart.
root_cause: Purchase POST route looks up default Inventory (debit) and Payable (credit) accounts by hardcoded codes ['1000','6000'] and ['2000','5000']. These are US/anglo conventions. SKR03 (German Standard Chart of Accounts) uses '3300' (Wareneingang) for purchases and '1600' (Verbindlichkeiten aus Lieferungen und Leistungen) for trade payables. Result: backend throws PAYABLE_ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND → 400 Bad Request when posting against SKR03-imported chart.
evidence: Dashka visual report from Network Response body: '{"error":"PAYABLE_ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND: Import SKR03 or set default posting accounts"}'. SKR03 CSV inspection confirms: code 1600 (Verbindlichkeiten aus L+L, LIABILITY) is the German trade payables account; code 3300 (Wareneingang, EXPENSE) is the German incoming-goods account. Neither code was in the original lookup arrays.
fix: Extend both code lookup arrays additively. Now: inventory matches ['1000','3300','6000'], payable matches ['1600','2000','5000']. Pattern remains unchanged otherwise.

checks.name: static — inventory lookup includes SKR03 3300
checks.what: Inventory code array must include '3300' alongside existing US codes
checks.url: bash -c 'grep -A 2 "Try to find default inventory" app/api/company/\[companyId\]/purchases/\[purchaseId\]/post/route.ts | grep -c "3300"'

checks.name: static — payable lookup includes SKR03 1600
checks.what: Payable code array must include '1600' alongside existing US codes
checks.url: bash -c 'grep -A 2 "Try to find default payables" app/api/company/\[companyId\]/purchases/\[purchaseId\]/post/route.ts | grep -c "1600"'

checks.name: static — Task 68B.5.2 header present per Standard v3.0
checks.what: Header block declares Task 68B.5.2 and preserves prior history (Task 68B.2 + Task 66)
checks.url: bash -c 'h=$(head -25 app/api/company/\[companyId\]/purchases/\[purchaseId\]/post/route.ts); echo "$h" | grep -c "Task 68B.5.2"; echo "$h" | grep -c "PRIOR HISTORY"'

checks.name: build — TypeScript scan clean
checks.what: Array literal extension — no type or interface changes
checks.url: echo 'SolarBox auto-runs this; expect ✅ No TypeScript errors'

checks.name: build — pnpm build green
checks.url: echo 'SolarBox auto-runs this; expect ✅ Build PASSED'

checks.name: runtime — Purchase POST succeeds against SKR03 chart
checks.what: UI: Open existing DRAFT Purchase with Supplier + Product + Qty + Price → click Post → status flips POSTED. No 400 PAYABLE_ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND.
checks.url: echo 'manual: Open purchase P-XXXX → Post button → confirm OK → status badge becomes POSTED'

checks.name: DB — POSTED row has snapshot fields populated
checks.what: purchase_documents row for posted purchase has all 5 snapshot fields filled
checks.url: echo 'manual SQL: SELECT id, status, "totalNetAmount", "totalVatAmount", "totalGrossAmount", "journalEntryId", "postedAt" FROM purchase_documents WHERE status=\'POSTED\' ORDER BY "postedAt" DESC LIMIT 1;'

checks.name: DB — Journal entry created and balanced (D = C)
checks.what: journal_lines for this purchase exist; sum(debit) equals sum(credit)
checks.url: echo 'manual SQL: SELECT "journalEntryId", SUM(debit) d, SUM(credit) c FROM journal_lines WHERE "journalEntryId" = (SELECT "journalEntryId" FROM purchase_documents WHERE status=\'POSTED\' ORDER BY "postedAt" DESC LIMIT 1) GROUP BY "journalEntryId";'

checks.name: DB — Journal uses correct SKR03 accounts
checks.what: Debit line hits account 3300 (Wareneingang); Credit line hits 1600 (Verbindlichkeiten)
checks.url: echo 'manual SQL: SELECT jl.debit, jl.credit, a.code, a.name FROM journal_lines jl JOIN accounts a ON a.id=jl."accountId" WHERE jl."journalEntryId" = (SELECT "journalEntryId" FROM purchase_documents WHERE status=\'POSTED\' ORDER BY "postedAt" DESC LIMIT 1);'

checks.name: DB — Warehouse stock_movement created
checks.what: stock_movements row inserted for posted purchase, qty matches
checks.url: echo 'manual SQL: SELECT * FROM stock_movements WHERE "sourceDocumentId" = (SELECT id FROM purchase_documents WHERE status=\'POSTED\' ORDER BY "postedAt" DESC LIMIT 1);'

checks.name: INVARIANT — GOLDEN POST snapshot consistency
checks.what: purchase_documents.totalGrossAmount equals SUM(journal_lines.debit) for this purchase
checks.url: echo 'manual SQL: SELECT p."totalGrossAmount" snapshot_gross, (SELECT SUM(jl.debit) FROM journal_lines jl WHERE jl."journalEntryId" = p."journalEntryId") journal_sum FROM purchase_documents p WHERE p.status=\'POSTED\' ORDER BY p."postedAt" DESC LIMIT 1;'

not_included: ProductSelector → Dialog UX (Leanid observation) — backlog Task 68B.8
Save & Post combined action — backlog Task 68B.9
Warehouse module verification — automatically validated by SQL check #11 above
diff_summary: +25 / -7 lines. Header expanded per Standard v3.0 (full history preserved). Two array literal extensions in helper function. Zero logic changes. Zero new abstractions.
